firm.name: Asset Valuation Group
firm.oneLiner: A Munich based M&A and corporate finance advisory boutique focused on mid-market transactions, real estate transactions, and company valuations.
fitNotes: Clear fit for an AI native deal platform because they run full process mandates, including planning, negotiation, signing, and closing.
They work across both M&A and real estate, so a shared workflow layer for documents, tasks, and buyer management could be useful.
They emphasize discretion and anonymity, which suggests value in controlled access, audit trails, and secure collaboration.
Their public site does not mention a data room or client portal, so there is room for modernizing the deal execution stack.

people.name: Robert Hang
people.role: Dipl.-Kfm. CFA, founder and managing director
people.focus: M&A, real estate transactions, corporate finance, company strategy

footprint.title: Founder and managing director
footprint.summary: The site says Asset Valuation Group was founded by Dipl.-Kfm. Robert Hang, CFA.

likelyPriorities: Likely cares about preserving a high touch boutique positioning.
Likely values tools that improve efficiency without reducing discretion.
Likely interested in better tracking of complex mandates and valuations.

signals.title: Brand reorientation to Valmont Partners
signals.detail: The homepage says the firm is rebranding after more than fifteen years and will operate as Valmont Partners from 1 January 2026.

signals.title: New M&A handbook available on Amazon
signals.detail: The homepage notes, "Our new M&A Handbook is available on Amazon."

signals.title: Track record updated through December 2022
signals.detail: The about page states the team's transaction volume figure is as of December 2022.

play.angle: Lead with the rebrand, because it is the clearest why now signal and suggests they may be revisiting how the firm presents itself and runs its process. Position Fintalo as a way to keep the boutique, high touch feel while reducing email and spreadsheet sprawl across NDA handling, buyer coordination, and document release. Avoid sounding like a heavy enterprise system or pushing a generic CRM pitch, since the firm’s positioning is discretion first and the likely concern is preserving that client experience.

objections.objection: We are a small boutique, so we do not need a full platform.
objections.counter: That is exactly where a lighter operating layer can help, especially if it replaces scattered email threads and manual tracking without changing how you run the deal.

objections.objection: Discretion is central for us, and we do not want a tool that adds exposure.
objections.counter: Understood, Fintalo is built for controlled access and stage gated sharing, so you can keep information release tight rather than broader.

objections.objection: We already manage deals well with email and Excel.
objections.counter: That works until a mandate gets more complex or time sensitive, and then the friction shows up in buyer follow up, version control, and status tracking. A short demo can show whether the workflow is actually simpler.

openingEmail.subject: Quick idea for Valmont Partners
openingEmail.body:
Hi Robert,

I saw your homepage note that Asset Valuation Group will operate as Valmont Partners from 1 January 2026. If you are revisiting the firm’s process alongside the rebrand, I thought it might be useful to show you Fintalo, an AI native M&A operating system that keeps NDA workflows, buyer management, and document release in one place.

It is built to preserve a boutique, discreet client experience while cutting down on email and Excel sprawl. Would you be open to a short demo next week?

Best,
openingEmail.groundedFact: I saw your homepage note that Asset Valuation Group will operate as Valmont Partners from 1 January 2026.

components.label: Service / sector fit
components.rationale: Core M&A / corporate-finance advisory: the bullseye.

components.label: Timeliness (why-now)
components.rationale: 3 live "why now" signal(s); 1 decision-maker(s) with a public footprint to reference
